free_text: Electric quadrupole strength in 16O from the
15N(p,gamma0)16O reaction
The model EN tandem Van de Graaff
accelerator at the University of Wisconsin-Madison
Nuclear Physics Laboratory.
Wisconsin colliding-beam polarized negative ion source
The capture gamma rays were detected in a
single cylindrical crystal of NaI(T1), 25 cm long by
25 cm in diameter. The crystal is surrounded by an
annulus of plastic scintillator (NE102) 10 cm thick.
The target gas cell consisted of a vertical cylindrical
steel chamber 3.81 cm in diameter. The target was N2
gas. The pressure averaged 400 mm Hg, corresponding to
an effective target thick ness of 2.7 mg/cm2.
All E1 and E2 transition matrix elements were
determined using a partial-wave analysis.
OG
No information on uncertainties. The error bars are
smaller than the dots presented in the graphs.
Fig.2 (p.2292) in Phys.Rev.C,37,2289,1988 was
digitized by GSYS2.4.7.
